description_long: This is a commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage, painted primarily white with blue and yellow accents and the 'British Caledonian' livery along the side. The aircraft features a low-mounted, swept-back wing configuration and two rear-mounted jet engines positioned on either side of the fuselage near the tail. The tail assembly is a T-tail design, with the horizontal stabilizer mounted atop the vertical fin. The windows are arranged in a single row along the fuselage, and the cockpit is located at the very front, distinguished by a blue nose cone and multiple cockpit windows. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall design is typical of short- to medium-range passenger jets from the late 20th century.
description: A white and blue commercial jet airliner with a T-tail, low-mounted swept wings, and rear-mounted engines, featuring 'British Caledonian' livery.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue nose cone, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical sweep of the wings and T-tail.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full 'British Caledonian' livery, row of passenger windows, and both rear-mounted engines.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the T-tail structure, engine exhausts, and the tapering fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the airline branding, windows, and engine placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from typical design)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
